science.claim: Bananas are technically berries, but strawberries are not.
science.explanation: In botanical terms, a berry develops from a single ovary. Bananas qualify; strawberries are 'accessory fruits' because the flesh comes from the receptacle, not the ovary.

science.claim: Glass is a liquid that flows very slowly over time.
science.explanation: This is a persistent myth. Glass is an amorphous solid. Old windowpanes are thicker at the bottom due to manufacturing processes, not flow.

science.claim: Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
science.explanation: Many fundamental cellular processes (metabolism, DNA replication) are shared across all life. Around 60% of human genes have recognizable counterparts in the banana genome.

science.claim: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
science.explanation: Lightning frequently strikes the same location. The Empire State Building is hit about 20-25 times per year. Tall structures and geographic features attract repeated strikes.

science.claim: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
science.explanation: Two branchial hearts pump blood through the gills, one systemic heart pumps it through the body. Their blood uses copper-based hemocyanin (blue) instead of iron-based hemoglobin (red).

science.claim: Water drains in opposite directions in the Northern and Southern hemispheres due to the Coriolis effect.
science.explanation: The Coriolis effect is far too weak to influence water draining from a sink or toilet. The drain direction is determined by basin geometry, residual currents, and initial water motion.

history.claim: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for his era.
history.explanation: Napoleon was about 5'7" (170 cm), average or slightly above average for French men of his time. The 'short' myth comes from British propaganda and confusion between French and English inches.

history.claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
history.explanation: Multiple astronauts have confirmed it is not visible from low Earth orbit without aid. The wall is narrow (15-30 feet wide) and blends with the surrounding terrain.

history.claim: Vikings wore horned helmets in battle.
history.explanation: No archaeological evidence supports horned Viking helmets. This image was popularized by 19th-century Romantic artists and later by opera costumes. Real Viking helmets were rounded iron or leather caps.

history.claim: Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
history.explanation: The Great Pyramid was built around 2560 BC. Cleopatra died in 30 BC (2530 years later). The Moon landing was 1969 AD (1999 years after Cleopatra). The pyramid predates Cleopatra by 500+ more years.

history.claim: Albert Einstein failed mathematics in school.
history.explanation: Einstein excelled at mathematics throughout school. He mastered calculus by age 15. This myth may stem from a misunderstanding of Swiss grading scales or conflation with other struggles.

history.claim: Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
history.explanation: Teaching at Oxford dates to at least 1096 AD. The Aztec Empire was founded with the establishment of Tenochtitlan in 1325 AD — over 200 years later.

geography.claim: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth measured from base to peak.
geography.explanation: Mauna Kea in Hawaii is the tallest from base to peak at ~10,203m (most is underwater). Everest has the highest elevation above sea level (8,849m), but its base-to-peak height is less.

geography.claim: Russia has a larger surface area than Pluto.
geography.explanation: Russia covers about 17.1 million km². Pluto's surface area is approximately 16.7 million km². Russia is slightly larger than the dwarf planet's entire surface.

geography.claim: The Sahara Desert is the largest desert on Earth.
geography.explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert at 14.2 million km² — a desert is defined by precipitation, not temperature. The Sahara is the largest hot desert at 9.2 million km².

geography.claim: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way.
geography.explanation: Earth has approximately 3 trillion trees. The Milky Way contains an estimated 100-400 billion stars. Trees outnumber our galaxy's stars by roughly 7-30x.

geography.claim: Africa is larger than China, the USA, India, and most of Europe combined.
geography.explanation: Africa's area is 30.37 million km². China (9.6M) + USA (9.8M) + India (3.3M) + Western Europe (~3.9M) = ~26.6M km². Africa is significantly larger.

geography.claim: The Amazon River is the longest river in the world.
geography.explanation: The Nile is generally considered the longest river at approximately 6,650 km. The Amazon is the largest by water volume and drainage basin, but shorter at approximately 6,400 km.

technology.claim: The first computer programmer was a woman named Ada Lovelace in the 1840s.
technology.explanation: Ada Lovelace wrote detailed notes on Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine in 1843, including what is recognized as the first algorithm intended for machine processing — making her the first computer programmer.

technology.claim: 5G cellular signals can interfere with human brain activity.
technology.explanation: 5G radio frequencies (sub-6 GHz and mmWave) are non-ionizing radiation, far too weak to affect neural activity. Extensive WHO and IEEE research confirms no health effects at standard power levels.

technology.claim: More computing power exists in a modern smartphone than was used for the Apollo 11 Moon landing.
technology.explanation: The Apollo Guidance Computer had ~74KB memory and ran at 0.043 MHz. A modern smartphone has billions of times more processing power and millions of times more memory.

technology.claim: Quantum computers can break all existing encryption instantly.
technology.explanation: Current quantum computers have too few stable qubits and too much noise. Shor's algorithm could theoretically break RSA, but would require millions of error-corrected qubits — far beyond current capability.

technology.claim: The QWERTY keyboard layout was designed to slow typists down.
technology.explanation: QWERTY was designed to prevent mechanical jamming in early typewriters by separating commonly paired letters. It was not intended to slow typing — it optimized mechanical reliability.

technology.claim: Nintendo existed as a company before the Eiffel Tower was built.
technology.explanation: Nintendo was founded on September 23, 1889 as a playing card company. The Eiffel Tower opened on March 31, 1889 — the same year, but Nintendo was actually incorporated slightly later. However, the founding family business dates to 1889.

finance.claim: The US dollar has lost over 95% of its purchasing power since the Federal Reserve was created in 1913.
finance.explanation: Due to cumulative inflation, $1 in 1913 has roughly the same purchasing power as $30+ today. This means the dollar has lost about 96-97% of its value, which is a normal consequence of moderate inflation over 110+ years.

finance.claim: Most actively managed mutual funds outperform their benchmark index over a 15-year period.
finance.explanation: According to S&P SPIVA data, roughly 87-92% of actively managed funds underperform their benchmark index over 15 years. This is one of the strongest arguments for passive index investing.

finance.claim: Credit card companies make most of their revenue from interest charges on carried balances.
finance.explanation: While interest is significant, interchange fees (charged to merchants on every transaction) are the largest revenue source for most card networks and issuers, especially for premium cards where cardholders often pay in full.

finance.claim: A penny doubled every day for 30 days would exceed $5 million.
finance.explanation: Starting with $0.01 and doubling daily: after 30 days you would have 2^29 cents = $5,368,709.12. This demonstrates the power of exponential growth, which humans consistently underestimate.

finance.claim: The stock market has historically gone up more often than down on any given year.
finance.explanation: The S&P 500 has posted positive annual returns roughly 73% of the time since 1928. Despite dramatic crashes, the long-term upward trend is remarkably consistent.

finance.claim: Cryptocurrency transactions are completely anonymous and untraceable.
finance.explanation: Bitcoin and most cryptocurrencies use public blockchains where every transaction is permanently recorded. Chain analysis firms like Chainalysis routinely trace transactions for law enforcement. Only specific privacy coins offer stronger anonymity.
